# Migration Consolidation (Postgrator baseline) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Replace the 44 re-run-every-boot SQL files with one verified baseline (`001.do.baseline.sql`) applied exactly once via Postgrator tracking.

**Architecture:** The baseline's content is generated by `pg_dump --schema-only` from the fully-migrated dev DB, curated for readability, and proven byte-equivalent to the legacy 44-file chain by a scratch-DB dump diff BEFORE the legacy files are deleted. `migrate()` keeps its signature and advisory lock but delegates to Postgrator (`schema_migrations` table, MD5 checksums, forward-only). The dev DB is dropped and recreated (approved data loss).

**Tech Stack:** Node 25 ESM, `pg`, `postgrator@8.0.0`, `node:test`, kubectl against docker-desktop (`deploy/postgres` in namespace `devproof`).

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-19-migration-consolidation-design.md`

## Global Constraints

- Windows host; run shell steps in **Git Bash** (Bash tool), not PowerShell, unless noted.
- Dev DB: `postgres://devproof:devproof-dev@127.0.0.1:15432/devproof` (localhost-lb → in-cluster `deploy/postgres`). In-pod `psql`/`pg_dump` as `-U postgres` use socket trust — no password.
- Scratchpad dir (throwaway artifacts, never committed): `C:\Users\carst\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--Users-carst-Desktop-devproofai\b9d7ef43-8131-4dd4-9d57-3d9e46256722\scratchpad` — referred to as `$SCRATCH` below.
- The baseline must be **semantically identical** to the legacy chain's end state — no schema changes ride along.
- Legacy files are deleted only after the verification diff is empty (Task 1 gates Task 2).
- Between Task 2's file swap and its DB reset step, do NOT boot the CP or run other tests — the intermediate state is intentionally not runnable against the old dev DB.
- Commit messages end with the project trailer:
  `Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>` + `Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01LmecgRnEhuMapvgbH5pYB6`

### Task 1: Generate and verify the baseline (scratchpad only — no repo changes, no commit)

**Files:**
- Create: `$SCRATCH/dump.sql` (raw pg_dump, working input)
- Create: `$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql` (the curated baseline — Task 2 copies it into the repo)
- Create: `$SCRATCH/verify.sh` (throwaway verification script)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: the live dev DB (all 44 legacy migrations applied), the legacy files `control-plane/sql/[0-9][0-9][0-9]_*.sql`
- Produces: `$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql` whose scratch-DB schema dump is **byte-identical** to the legacy chain's, plus matching seed rows. Task 2 consumes this file verbatim.

- [ ] **Step 1: Dump the end-state schema from the live dev DB**

```bash
kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- pg_dump -U postgres --schema-only --no-owner --no-privileges devproof > "$SCRATCH/dump.sql"
wc -l "$SCRATCH/dump.sql"   # expect several hundred lines
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Curate `dump.sql` into `$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql`**

Editing rules (readability edits only — never change semantics):
- **Remove:** the `SET ...` / `SELECT pg_catalog.set_config...` preamble, `pg_dump` banner comments (`-- Dumped from...`, `-- Name: ...; Type: ...` headers), any `ALTER ... OWNER TO` lines, and `\restrict`/`\unrestrict` lines if the pg_dump version emits them.
- **Strip** `public.` qualifiers (both sides dump back qualified, so the diff still matches).
- **Keep verbatim:** every `CREATE FUNCTION` body, every `CREATE TRIGGER`, every explicit constraint/index name exactly as dumped. Where pg_dump splits a table into `CREATE TABLE` + later `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT`, you MAY inline PK/UNIQUE/FK/DEFAULT into the `CREATE TABLE` only when the auto-generated name Postgres would pick (`<table>_pkey`, `<table>_<col>_key`, `<table>_<col>_fkey`, `<table>_<col>_check`) equals the dumped name — otherwise keep the dump's two-statement form. When unsure, keep the dump's form; the diff in Step 4 is the referee.
- **Reorder** into domain groups with a one-line header comment each (tables may be reordered freely because pg_dump emits FK constraints as trailing ALTERs — keep all `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY` statements after all `CREATE TABLE`s): workspaces/settings → agents/sessions/events → files/skills/memory/wikis → environments/vaults → serving (external_deployments, model_routing, routings, wake_requests, pending_launches) → usage/billing (gateway_usage, cost_entries, resource_prices, routing_rejects) → webhooks/keys.
- **Carry forward load-bearing comments** from the legacy files, e.g. why `uq_session_events_uid` is partial (`WHERE uid IS NOT NULL` — uid-less legacy events insert unconditionally), the 027-trigger sole-writer rule on `sessions.tokens_in/out`, the workspace-name partial unique index over non-deleted rows.
- **Append the two seed inserts** at the end:

```sql
-- Seeds: the immutable default workspace and the settings singleton.
INSERT INTO workspaces (id, name) VALUES ('wrkspc_default', 'Default workspace')
  ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
INSERT INTO app_settings (id) VALUES ('global') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
```

(Check the legacy `010_workspaces.sql` / `030_cost_tracking.sql` for the exact original conflict clauses and mirror them.)
- Use **LF line endings** throughout.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write `$SCRATCH/verify.sh`**

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Throwaway: proves the curated baseline == legacy 44-file chain. Never committed.
set -euo pipefail
SCRATCH="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO="/c/Users/carst/Desktop/devproofai"
PSQL="kubectl exec -i deploy/postgres -n devproof -- psql -U postgres -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1"

kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- psql -U postgres \
  -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS verify_legacy" -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS verify_baseline" \
  -c "CREATE DATABASE verify_legacy" -c "CREATE DATABASE verify_baseline"

# Legacy chain, same order as the old migrate(): ASCII filename sort.
for f in $(ls "$REPO"/control-plane/sql/[0-9][0-9][0-9]_*.sql | sort); do
  $PSQL -d verify_legacy -f - < "$f" > /dev/null || { echo "LEGACY FAILED: $f"; exit 1; }
done
$PSQL -d verify_baseline -f - < "$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql" > /dev/null

kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- pg_dump -U postgres --schema-only --no-owner --no-privileges verify_legacy   > "$SCRATCH/legacy.dump"
kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- pg_dump -U postgres --schema-only --no-owner --no-privileges verify_baseline > "$SCRATCH/baseline.dump"

echo "--- schema diff (must be empty) ---"
diff "$SCRATCH/legacy.dump" "$SCRATCH/baseline.dump"

echo "--- seed diff (must be empty) ---"
$PSQL -d verify_legacy   -c "SELECT id, name FROM workspaces ORDER BY id" -c "SELECT id FROM app_settings" > "$SCRATCH/legacy.seeds"
$PSQL -d verify_baseline -c "SELECT id, name FROM workspaces ORDER BY id" -c "SELECT id FROM app_settings" > "$SCRATCH/baseline.seeds"
diff "$SCRATCH/legacy.seeds" "$SCRATCH/baseline.seeds"

kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- psql -U postgres \
  -c "DROP DATABASE verify_legacy" -c "DROP DATABASE verify_baseline"
echo "VERIFIED: baseline == legacy chain"
```

Both dumps come from the same in-pod `pg_dump` binary, so dump artifacts are identical on both sides — a plain `diff` is the normalizer. Do NOT add filtering to make a diff pass; fix the baseline instead (name a constraint explicitly if an auto-name differs).

- [ ] **Step 4: Run it; iterate on the baseline until both diffs are empty**

```bash
bash "$SCRATCH/verify.sh"
```

Expected final output: both `--- ... ---` sections print nothing between them, then `VERIFIED: baseline == legacy chain`. Typical first-pass differences: constraint-name mismatches from over-eager inlining (revert to the dump's two-statement form), missed `DEFAULT`s, sequence/identity details. Re-run after each fix.

- [ ] **Step 5: Record evidence**

Keep `$SCRATCH/legacy.dump`, `$SCRATCH/baseline.dump`, and the clean run output for the Task 2 gate. No commit — nothing in the repo changed.

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql` (verified in Task 1).
- Produces: `migrate(pool: pg.Pool): Promise<void>` — same exported signature as today; all ~21 existing callers (main.ts + test files) are untouched. New DB table `schema_migrations` (Postgrator-managed: `version BIGINT PK, name, md5, run_at`).

- [ ] **Step 1: Gate — confirm Task 1's verification passed**

Re-run `bash "$SCRATCH/verify.sh"` if there is any doubt the baseline changed since. Do not proceed on a non-empty diff.

- [ ] **Step 2: Install postgrator**

```bash
cd control-plane && npm install postgrator@8.0.0
```

Expected: `package.json` gains `"postgrator": "^8.0.0"` (or `8.0.0`), lockfile updated.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the line-ending rule**

```
control-plane/sql/*.sql text eol=lf
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Write the failing test `control-plane/test/migrate.test.ts`**

```ts
// Tracked migrations (Postgrator): baseline applies once, re-run is a no-op,
// checksum tamper fails the boot (migration-consolidation spec 2026-07-19).
import { test } from "node:test";
import assert from "node:assert/strict";
import { createPool, migrate } from "../src/db.ts";

const pool = createPool();
let available = true;
try { await pool.query("SELECT 1"); await migrate(pool); } catch { available = false; }

test("baseline is applied and tracked in schema_migrations", { skip: !available }, async () => {
  // Postgrator's ensureTable() seeds a permanent version-0 sentinel row
  // ("nothing applied yet") — real migrations start at version 1.
  const versions = await pool.query("SELECT version, md5 FROM schema_migrations WHERE version > 0 ORDER BY version");
  assert.ok(versions.rows.length >= 1, "at least the baseline row");
  assert.equal(Number(versions.rows[0].version), 1);
  const t = await pool.query("SELECT to_regclass('workspaces') AS t");
  assert.ok(t.rows[0].t, "baseline created the schema");
});

test("second migrate() is a no-op", { skip: !available }, async () => {
  const before = await pool.query("SELECT count(*)::int AS n, max(version)::int AS v FROM schema_migrations");
  await migrate(pool);
  const after = await pool.query("SELECT count(*)::int AS n, max(version)::int AS v FROM schema_migrations");
  assert.deepEqual(after.rows[0], before.rows[0]);
});

test("checksum mismatch on an applied migration fails migrate()", { skip: !available }, async () => {
  const { rows: [orig] } = await pool.query("SELECT md5 FROM schema_migrations WHERE version = 1");
  await pool.query("UPDATE schema_migrations SET md5 = 'tampered' WHERE version = 1");
  try {
    await assert.rejects(() => migrate(pool), /checksum|md5/i);
  } finally {
    // Restore even on assertion failure — a wrong stored md5 would fail every later boot.
    await pool.query("UPDATE schema_migrations SET md5 = $1 WHERE version = 1", [orig.md5]);
  }
});
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Run it to verify it fails**

```bash
cd control-plane && node --test --test-concurrency=1 test/migrate.test.ts
```

Expected: FAIL — old `migrate()` succeeds but `relation "schema_migrations" does not exist` in test 1.

- [ ] **Step 6: Swap the sql directory**

```bash
cp "$SCRATCH/001.do.baseline.sql" control-plane/sql/001.do.baseline.sql
git rm control-plane/sql/[0-9][0-9][0-9]_*.sql
ls control-plane/sql   # expect exactly: 001.do.baseline.sql
```

- [ ] **Step 7: Rewrite `migrate()` in `control-plane/src/db.ts`**

Replace lines 15–37 (the current `migrate` function) with:

```ts
import Postgrator from "postgrator";
```
(import goes at the top, next to the existing imports; `readdirSync` becomes unused — remove it from the `node:fs` import, keep `readFileSync` only if still used, else drop the whole import)

```ts
export async function migrate(pool: pg.Pool): Promise<void> {
  const dir = fileURLToPath(new URL("../sql", import.meta.url));
  // Tracked migrations (Postgrator, spec 2026-07-19): sql/*.sql run exactly once,
  // recorded in schema_migrations with an MD5 per file — editing an applied file
  // fails the boot (validateChecksums). Forward-only; no undo files.
  // Concurrent migrators (parallel test files; multi-replica boots) still race
  // DDL, and Postgrator does no locking of its own — the database-wide advisory
  // lock below remains what serializes them. Session-level (not xact) because
  // migrations run as their own implicit transactions on this one client.
  const client = await pool.connect();
  try {
    await client.query("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(hashtext('devproof_migrate'))");
    const { rows: [{ db }] } = await client.query("SELECT current_database() AS db");
    const postgrator = new Postgrator({
      migrationPattern: dir.replaceAll("\\", "/") + "/*",
      driver: "pg",
      database: db,
      schemaTable: "schema_migrations",
      validateChecksums: true,
      newline: "LF", // normalize CRLF before MD5 so Windows/Linux checkouts agree
      execQuery: (query) => client.query(query),
    });
    await postgrator.migrate();
  } finally {
    try {
      await client.query("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(hashtext('devproof_migrate'))");
      client.release();
    } catch (err) {
      client.release(err as Error); // unlock failed ⇒ connection is suspect: destroy, don't pool it
    }
  }
}
```

If `npx tsc --noEmit` reports missing type declarations for `postgrator` (v8 ships `postgrator.d.ts`, so this is unlikely), create `control-plane/src/postgrator.d.ts`:

```ts
declare module "postgrator" {
  export default class Postgrator {
    constructor(options: {
      migrationPattern: string; driver: string; database: string;
      schemaTable?: string; validateChecksums?: boolean; newline?: string;
      execQuery: (query: string) => Promise<{ rows: unknown[] }>;
    });
    migrate(target?: string): Promise<unknown[]>;
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 8: Stop anything using the dev DB, then drop and recreate it (approved data loss)**

First confirm the CP is not running (port 7080; note: German netstat prints `ABHÖREN`, not `LISTENING`):

```powershell
netstat -ano | findstr :7080   # expect no listener; if one exists, stop that process first
```

Then (WITH (FORCE) kicks out the gateway pods' pooled connections; they reconnect on their own):

```bash
kubectl exec deploy/postgres -n devproof -- psql -U postgres -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 \
  -c "DROP DATABASE devproof WITH (FORCE)" \
  -c "CREATE DATABASE devproof OWNER devproof"
```

Expected: `DROP DATABASE` / `CREATE DATABASE`. Consequences accepted per spec: all dev rows gone; MinIO objects orphaned (GC sweep's job); external `dpk_` keys gone (gateway 401s external clients until a new key is created via the console).

- [ ] **Step 9: Run the migrate test to verify it passes**

```bash
cd control-plane && node --test --test-concurrency=1 test/migrate.test.ts
```

Expected: PASS (3/3). The first `migrate(pool)` at import applies the baseline to the fresh DB.

- [ ] **Step 10: Full suite + typecheck**

```bash
cd control-plane && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test
```

Expected: tsc clean; suite green (the workspace sweep at the end runs as usual). If a test fails on a missing-row assumption, check whether it depended on legacy re-run semantics before touching it — that is a finding to fix in the test, not by re-adding idempotency to sql files.

- [ ] **Step 11: Commit (one atomic commit — code, sql swap, test, deps, gitattributes)**

```bash
git add .gitattributes control-plane/package.json control-plane/package-lock.json \
  control-plane/src/db.ts control-plane/test/migrate.test.ts control-plane/sql/001.do.baseline.sql
git add -u control-plane/sql
git commit -m "feat(db): consolidate 44 migrations into Postgrator-tracked baseline

Baseline generated from pg_dump of the fully-migrated schema and verified
byte-identical to the legacy chain via scratch-DB dump diff before deletion.
migrate() keeps its signature + advisory lock; files now run exactly once,
checksum-validated (editing an applied file fails the boot). Dev DB reset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01LmecgRnEhuMapvgbH5pYB6"
```

### Task 3: Live verification + cluster leftovers (no repo changes)

**Files:** none (operational task)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: Task 2's reset DB + new baseline.
- Produces: a verified-running dev environment; stale per-session `-work` PVCs removed.

- [ ] **Step 1: Boot the CP against the fresh DB**

Per CLAUDE.md (NOT `npm run dev`):

```bash
cd control-plane && DEVPROOF_RUNNER_IMAGE=devproof/devproofai-session-runner:dev50 \
DEVPROOF_S3_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:19000 DEVPROOF_S3_BUCKET=devproof-files \
DEVPROOF_S3_ACCESS_KEY=devproof DEVPROOF_S3_SECRET_KEY=devproof-dev-secret \
DEVPROOF_GATEWAY_NAMESPACE=devproof DEVPROOF_SERVING_NAMESPACE=devproof \
npx tsx src/main.ts
```

(run in background; watch the log). Expected: boots clean — baseline applies (`schema_migrations` row 1), internal key recreated, no migration errors.

- [ ] **Step 2: Console up + pages 200**

If the console isn't already running: `cd console && npx next build && npx next start -p 7090` (production build, per CLAUDE.md; restart if it was running before a rebuild). Then:

```bash
for p in / /sessions /agents /environments /vaults /memory /wikis /skills /files /workspaces /deployments /catalog /pools /routings /keys /usage /settings /webhooks; do
  printf "%-16s %s\n" "$p" "$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:7090$p)"
done
```

Expected: all 200. (Exact nav paths: cross-check `console/app` route dirs if one 404s — a 404 from a wrong path in this list is not a regression.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Exercise a workspace-scoped write flow live**

```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:7080/v1/workspaces -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"name":"smoke-post-reset"}'
curl -s http://localhost:7080/v1/workspaces | head -c 400
```

Expected: create returns a `wrkspc_` id; list shows `wrkspc_default` + the new one. Then delete it (`DELETE /v1/workspaces/:id` → 202) to leave the environment clean.

- [ ] **Step 4: Remove per-session `-work` PVCs orphaned by the reset**

```bash
kubectl get pvc -n devproof -o name | grep -E 'sesn.*-work' || echo "none"
# for each listed: kubectl delete -n devproof <pvc>
```

Expected: leftovers (if any) deleted; `devproof-pg-data`, MinIO, and `*-model-cache` PVCs are NOT touched.

### Task 4: Documentation (CLAUDE.md)

**Files:**
- Modify: `CLAUDE.md` (repo root — the project file, not the global one)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: the shipped behavior from Task 2.
- Produces: accurate migration-model docs for future sessions.

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite the two stale migration passages**

1. In the `control-plane/` component bullet: replace the parenthetical starting `(`sql/NNN_*.sql`, latest `034`; `migrate()` re-runs EVERY sql file each boot ...)` — the whole description of the no-tracking re-run model through `...never recreate what later ones drop` — with:

> Postgres-backed (`sql/` = Postgrator-tracked migrations, table `schema_migrations`; baseline `001.do.baseline.sql` is the full schema, consolidated 2026-07-19 from the pre-release 44-file chain and dump-diff-verified). Files are named `NNN.do.<description>.sql`, run EXACTLY ONCE, forward-only (no undo files), and are IMMUTABLE once applied — Postgrator stores an MD5 per file and fails the boot if an applied file changes (`newline: "LF"` + `.gitattributes` keep checksums stable across platforms). New migrations: next number, plain `ALTER TABLE`, no idempotency or backfill guards needed. The advisory lock in `migrate()` still serializes concurrent migrators.

2. In the **Last-modified (migration 035)** bullet: the final sentence about backfill guards being load-bearing because `migrate()` re-runs every file each boot — rewrite to note the guards are gone with the baseline consolidation (run-once model; historical context only).

Also scan CLAUDE.md for other `migration NNN` cross-references (`migration 029`, `020`, `026`, `027`, `031`, `033`, `035`, `036`, `037`, `039`, `041`, `042`, `043`, `044`, `022`, `025`, `028`…): keep them as historical spec pointers (they document WHEN a column arrived, still true) — do not renumber; where one implies a live file (e.g. "see 017/022"), note the files now live only in git history.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the test-runner note still holds**

Confirm the `--test-concurrency=1` paragraph: race (1) (migrate-DDL deadlock) is now moot, race (2) (`app_settings` singleton) is not — the flag stays. Update the paragraph's race-(1) description to past tense/moot rather than deleting it.
